codeReview.summary: Runtime OHLCV sync and backfill now invoke gap repair after persisting fetched candles, and the added runtime test proves an interior gap is closed end-to-end once upstream data becomes available.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature was assigned to core-api-worker even though the changes are runtime-provider coordination work in the OHLCV runtime/backfill lifecycle. The dedicated runtime-provider-worker skill already documents the relevant runtime tracing and live-probe procedure, so feature-to-skill mapping appears misaligned.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:1567 assigns `skillName: "core-api-worker"` for this runtime wiring feature, while `.factory/skills/runtime-provider-worker/SKILL.md` says it should be used for provider/runtime coordination, startup lifecycle, and runtime-state changes.

summary: Pass. The reviewed commit wires gap repair into both runtime sync and backfill flows and adds end-to-end runtime coverage showing the gap-repair loop closes once missing upstream candles appear. No blocking code issues found; one shared-state observation notes the feature was routed through core-api-worker instead of the more specific runtime-provider-worker skill.
